Accordingly, the registration of the students, who did not meet with the criteria fixed by the afore mentioned rule, Wa5 refused even though they had successfully passed 8 Class examination This resulted into filing of constitutional petitions on behalf of such students Initiدlly, learned Single Judge ofthis Court dismissed Writ Petition No 17732 0f 2012 0n 19.03.2013. However, unolher leurned Judge subsequently took contrary view while deciding Writ Petition No.14240 of 20!2 17,12.2015 (agwin5t which order the present is filed) und the students who hud pussed 8 " class examination but had not yet attained the age of 12 years were allowed to be registered for Class IX examination In view ofthe conflicting decisions on the issue, leدrned Judge of this Court on 06O5 2Oi6 passed the following order in Writ Petition No 14832/2016: fhe Jirst Juagmienr passed tn WP No 17732/12 r٤li٤ h45 bee rejised 1& touc hJtone I7٤ Single Single uppeal Single

eligibility criteria for admission to Class-IX Under the rules student secking admission in Class-IX must be of 12 years of age at the time of his registration with the Board The Board registers the students on 1 August of every year The relevant rule (Rule 1-A) Was framed on 28.052Ol3 and ١٥٦ introduced in the Calendar by the Boards on 1OlO2Oi3 In uddition to above it was submiued that Article 25٠A of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 19T3 (the - Constitution provides that State shall provide free and compulsory ecucation to all children of the age of five to sixteen ears in such manner as may be detemmined by law It was stated that the Punjab Education Code (PEC") framed by the Govemment of Punjab ordains that the age of every child for initial admission in school for Class shall be five years and the maximum age for aumission to Classes XI and XIII in shall be 20 ears and 25 years respectively ( Article 29) It was thus argued before us that studenit who to be aumitied in Class at the age of tive years cannot possibly be registered in Class IX without crossing Ihe barrier of 13 years of age However, into account Ihe possibility that one-time double
